docs.title: Fraggle
docs.text: Fraggle is a Kotlin-based AI assistant framework that integrates with messaging platforms using local LLM providers.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• ReAct-style Agent Loop - Iterative reasoning with tool calling
  • Signal Integration - Chat via Signal messenger
  • Extensible Skill System - DSL-based skill definitions
  • Hierarchical Memory - Global, chat, and user-scoped memory
  • Local LLM Support - Works with LM Studio and compatible providers

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Clone and build
git clone https://github.com/DrewCarlson/Fraggle.git
cd Fraggle
./gradlew build

# Run interactive chat mode
./gradlew :app:run --args="chat"

See the Getting Started guide for full setup instructions.

docs.title: Agent System
docs.text:
Work in Progress

This documentation is being written.

docs.title: ReAct Loop
docs.text: The FraggleAgent implements a ReAct-style (Reasoning + Acting) loop that iteratively processes messages and executes tools until a final response is generated.

docs.title: Memory System
docs.text:
Work in Progress

This documentation is being written.

docs.title: Memory Scopes
docs.text:
Fraggle supports three memory scopes:

  • Global - Shared across all conversations
  • Chat - Per-conversation memory
  • User - Per-user memory

Memory is persisted as human-readable markdown files.

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Work in Progress

This documentation is being written.

docs.title: Module Structure
docs.text:
Fraggle is organized into several Gradle modules:

  • fraggle - Core framework and abstractions
  • fraggle-signal - Signal messenger integration
  • fraggle-skills - Built-in skill implementations
  • app - CLI application
  • backend - Optional REST API server

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
Work in Progress

This documentation is being written.

docs.title: Skill DSL
docs.text:
Skills are defined using a Kotlin DSL:

skill("my_skill") {
    description = "Does something useful"

    parameter<String>("input") {
        description = "The input value"
        required = true
    }

    execute { params ->
        val input = params.get<String>("input")
        SkillResult.Success("Processed: $input")
    }
}

Work in Progress

This documentation is being written.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• JDK 21 or later
  • Gradle (wrapper included)

docs.title: Build Commands
docs.text:
# Build all modules
./gradlew build

# Build distribution
./gradlew :app:installDist

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository
  2. Open in IntelliJ IDEA or your preferred IDE
  3. Run ./gradlew build to verify setup

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text: The project uses standard Kotlin code style conventions.

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
Work in Progress

This documentation is being written.

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
# Run all tests
./gradlew test

# Run tests for a specific module
./gradlew :fraggle:test

# Run a specific test class
./gradlew :fraggle:test --tests="*SkillTest"

docs.title: Test Framework
docs.text: Tests use JUnit 5 with MockK for mocking and kotlinx-coroutines-test for coroutine testing.

docs.title: Configuration File
docs.text: Fraggle uses YAML configuration located at {FRAGGLE_ROOT}/config/fraggle.yaml.

docs.text: Variable Description Default FRAGGLE_ROOT Root directory for all Fraggle files Current directory

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Work in Progress

This documentation is being written.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• JDK 21 or later
  • signal-cli (for Signal integration)
  • LM Studio or compatible LLM provider

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/DrewCarlson/Fraggle.git
cd Fraggle
./gradlew build

docs.title: Running
docs.text:
# Interactive chat mode (for testing)
./gradlew :app:run --args="chat"

# Full service with Signal
./gradlew :app:run --args="run"

docs.title: Signal Setup
docs.text:
Work in Progress

This documentation is being written.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• signal-cli installed and in PATH
  • A phone number registered with Signal

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configure Signal in fraggle.yaml:

fraggle:
  bridges:
    signal:
      enabled: true
      phone: "+1234567890"
      trigger: "@fraggle"
